desc: Beppu is Japan's flagship hot-spring city, where weather matters less than choosing the right onsen district and balancing steam baths, coast views, and nearby mountain hikes.
hls: Hundreds of onsen options from sand baths to mud pools
Easy rail links to Yufuin and central Kyushu
Good winter shoulder value outside Japanese holiday peaks

todo.name: Beppu Hells circuit
todo.desc: Visit the seven jigoku geothermal sites early before tour buses arrive.

todo.name: Takegawara sand bath
todo.desc: Book a sand-bath slot and follow with a neighborhood sento crawl.

todo.name: Kitsuki samurai district day trip
todo.desc: Use a half day to explore preserved Edo-era streets in nearby Kitsuki.

todo.name: Mt. Tsurumi ropeway
todo.desc: Ride for bay views and cooler air, especially in warmer months.

todo.name: Jigoku-mushi cooking workshop
todo.desc: Try steam-cooked local produce and seafood using geothermal vents.

todo.name: Kannawa evening walk
todo.desc: Explore steam vents and small ryokan lanes after dark.

wifi.note: City hotels are dependable; smaller ryokan may have weaker room-level coverage.

prac.fltNote: No nonstop UK/Ireland flights to Oita; most routes connect via Tokyo, Osaka, Seoul, or Taipei, with strongest seat supply during spring blossom and autumn foliage periods.
prac.currency: Japan local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Some small bathhouses still restrict tattoos; confirm policy before booking.
Ryokan dinner cut-off times are strict, so avoid late-arrival rail plans on check-in day.
Typhoon remnants in late summer can disrupt ferries and local buses for a day or two.

sweet: Oct-Nov and Mar-May for pleasant walking weather; Jan-Feb is great for onsen focus with lower crowds.
